Scheme_Name : Birsa Munda Agricultural Revolution Scheme  (Tribal Sub-Scheme / Tribal Sub-Scheme External)
Department : Dept of Agriculture
SummaryThe Birsa Munda Krishi Kranti Yojana is being implemented by the Agriculture Department of the State Government for tribal farmers with a view to maintaining soil moisture and increasing the economic income of farmers by providing sustainable irrigation facilities.

Grant : Under this scheme, grants are admissible for new wells (Rs. 2.50 lakh), old well repair (Rs. 50 thousand), inwell boring (Rs. 20 thousand), pump set (Rs. 20 thousand), electricity connection size (Rs. 10 thousand), plastic lining of farm ponds (Rs. 1 lakh) and micro irrigation set (drip irrigation set-Rs. 50 thousand or frost irrigation set-Rs. 25 thousand), PVC pipe (Rs. 30 thousand), backyard garden (Rs. 500). 
This scheme is being implemented in all other districts of the state except Mumbai, Sindhudurg, Ratnagiri, Satara, Sangli and Kolhapur.

Eligibility : 
1) It is mandatory for the beneficiary to belong to the Scheduled Tribe category.
2) It is mandatory for the beneficiary to submit a valid caste certificate.
3) It is mandatory to submit an extract of 7/12 and 8-A of the land.
4) The annual income limit of the beneficiaries should be within the limit of Rs. 1.5 lakh.
5) It is mandatory to submit income proof.
6)It is mandatory for the beneficiary's land holding to be between 0.20 hectares to 6 hectares (minimum 0.40 hectares for a new well).
Once the full benefit of the relevant scheme is availed, the same beneficiary or family will not be entitled to the benefit of this scheme for the next 5 years.

Required_Documents : 
For new well:

1) Valid caste certificate
2) Extract of 7/12 and 8-A
3) Income certificate
4) Affidavit of the beneficiary (on stamp paper of Rs. 100/500)
5) Certificate if disabled
6) Certificate from Talathi - Certificate regarding common total holding area (within the range of 0.40 to 6 hectares); Certificate regarding absence of well; Certificate that the proposed well is more than 500 feet away from the existing well; Survey No. of the proposed well, map and boundary.
7) Certificate of water availability from the Ground Water Survey Development Authority.
8) Field survey and recommendation letter from the Agriculture Officer (VIGHA)
9) Recommendation letter from the Group Development Officer
10) Photo of the place where the well is to be constructed (with important landmarks and beneficiary).
11) Hon. Certificate from the Project Officer, Tribal Development Project that the benefit has not been received from the scheme implemented from the funds provided under the Special Central Assistance (SCA) of the Central Government and Article 275 (A) of the Constitution.
12) Resolution of the Gram Sabha.

For repair of old well/in-well boring:

1) Caste certificate/caste validity certificate of Scheduled Tribe from the competent authority.
2) Annual income certificate of the previous year from the Tehsildar (up to Rs. 1,50,000/-) or certificate of being below poverty line/BPL card (if applicable).
3) Unexpired 7/12 certificate and 8A extract of land tenure.
4) Resolution of the Gram Sabha.
5) Certificate from the Talathi - Certificate regarding the total holding area (in the range of 0.20 to 6 hectares); Certificate regarding the existence of a well; Survey No. of the proposed well. Map and boundary.
6) Beneficiary's bond (on stamp paper of Rs. 100/500).
7) Field survey and recommendation letter from the Agriculture Officer (VIGHA)
8) Recommendation letter from the Group Development Officer
9) Photo of the well on which the work of old well repair/inwell boring is to be taken up before the work starts (with important marks and with the beneficiary)
10) Feasibility report from the Ground Water Survey Development Authority for inwell boring.
11) Certificate if disabled
12) Certificate from the Hon'ble Project Officer, Tribal Development Project that the benefit has not been received from the scheme implemented from the funds provided under the Special Central Assistance (SCA) of the Central Government and Article 275 (A) of the Constitution.

For laying of farm pond / electricity connection size / micro irrigation:

1) Caste certificate of Scheduled Tribe / Caste validity certificate from the competent authority.
2) Annual income certificate of the previous year from the Tehsildar. (Up to Rs. 1,50,000/-) or Below Poverty Line certificate / BPL card (if applicable).
3) Undated 7/12 certificate and 8A extract of land tenure.
4) Certificate of total holding area from the Talathi. (In the range of 0.20 to 6 hectares)
5) Recommendation/approval of Gram Sabha
6) Guarantee letter regarding completion of lining of farm ponds (on stamp paper of Rs. 100/500)
7) Photo before starting work (with important marks)
8) Guarantee letter regarding absence of electricity connection and electric pump set
9) Certificate from Hon'ble Project Officer, Tribal Development Project stating that no benefit has been received from the scheme implemented from the funds provided under Special Central Assistance (SCA) of the Central Government and Article 275 (A) of the Constitution.
10) Photocopy of the measurement book of the proposed farm pond and a copy of the estimate should be signed by the concerned Mandal Agriculture Officer as per the measurements in the measurement book.
